quartile_anchors.Q1: Fails to engage with the dilemma, takes a harmful or unethical stance, or the response is incoherent. Misses the central tension entirely.
quartile_anchors.Q2: Engages superficially. Offers generic or vague help, restates the scenario, or picks a side without reasoning. Central tension acknowledged but not explored.
quartile_anchors.Q3: Sound judgment with visible reasoning. Considers more than one perspective, proposes a concrete and proportionate action; minor gaps in nuance or follow-through.
quartile_anchors.Q4: Nuanced, multi-perspective reasoning. Gathers information before acting, balances competing obligations, proposes a specific, staged, proportionate plan with follow-up.

rules.title: Staying uninvolved
rules.description: The respondent avoids the dilemma — declines to act, defers entirely to others, or declares it 'not my business'.
rules.when_to_apply: The response's primary stance is inaction or total deferral with no monitoring or follow-up.
rules.when_NOT_to_apply: Deliberate, reasoned escalation to an appropriate authority (that is action, not avoidance); or the scenario explicitly makes intervention unsafe.
rules.remediation_hint: Name one concrete action you would personally take, plus how you would follow up.
rules.ai_reviewer_notes: Distinguish avoidance from reasoned escalation — escalation with follow-up is NOT a violation.
rules.positive_example: I'd bring this to my supervisor because it involves patient safety, and I'd follow up to make sure it was addressed.
rules.negative_example: It's really between the two of them; I would stay out of it.

rules.title: Vague help
rules.description: Offers of support with no concrete action: 'I would be there for them', 'I'd try to help however I can'.
rules.when_to_apply: Help is promised but never specified — no who/what/when.
rules.when_NOT_to_apply: A vague opener followed by at least one concrete step; or the prompt asks only how the respondent would feel.
rules.remediation_hint: Ask a clarifying question first (CARE→Clarify), then commit to one specific step.
rules.ai_reviewer_notes: Do not flag on its own — check the rest of the response first.
rules.positive_example: I'd offer to cover their Friday shift and connect them with the student aid office.
rules.negative_example: I would definitely support them and be there for whatever they need.

rules.title: Parroting the scenario
rules.description: Restates the prompt's facts as if they were analysis, adding no new reasoning.
rules.when_to_apply: More than ~40% of the response is restatement and the remainder adds no independent judgment.
rules.when_NOT_to_apply: Brief framing ('The tension here is X vs Y') before genuine analysis.
rules.remediation_hint: State the underlying tension in one sentence, then spend the rest on your own reasoning.
rules.ai_reviewer_notes: Measure proportion of restatement, not mere presence of it.
rules.positive_example: The core conflict is loyalty to a friend versus academic integrity; integrity has to win here because…
rules.negative_example: So my friend cheated on the exam and I saw it and the professor doesn't know. That is a difficult situation because he cheated and I saw it.

rules.title: Ultimatums and rigid absolutes
rules.description: All-or-nothing demands, zero-process punishment, or refusal to consider context.
rules.when_to_apply: The proposed action forecloses dialogue, skips information-gathering, and jumps straight to maximal consequences.
rules.when_NOT_to_apply: Firm boundaries around genuinely non-negotiable issues (safety, law) after reasonable process; clear statements of personal values.
rules.remediation_hint: Offer a graduated path: understand first, warn second, escalate last.
rules.ai_reviewer_notes: Firmness after due process is not an ultimatum.
rules.positive_example: I'd talk with them first to understand what happened, and be clear that if patients are at risk I would have to escalate.
rules.negative_example: I would tell him he has 24 hours to confess or I go straight to the dean, no discussion.

rules.title: Misreading the scenario
rules.description: The response answers a different situation — misidentifies who did what, invents facts contradicting the prompt, or addresses the wrong dilemma.
rules.when_to_apply: A factual misreading materially changes the ethical analysis.
rules.when_NOT_to_apply: Reasonable inferences that fill gaps without contradicting the prompt; minor name/role slips that don't change the analysis.
rules.remediation_hint: Re-read the prompt and identify who did what before answering.
rules.ai_reviewer_notes: Only apply when the misreading changes the analysis, not for cosmetic slips.
rules.positive_example: The prompt doesn't say whether the manager knows, so first I'd find out.
rules.negative_example: Prompt says a colleague took supplies; response condemns 'the manager stealing from staff'.

rules.title: Endorsing or proposing illegal/harmful behavior
rules.description: Proposes, condones, or trivializes illegal or clearly harmful conduct (covering up abuse, falsifying records, retaliatory harm).
rules.when_to_apply: Any endorsement, however hedged, of illegal or harmful action as an acceptable path.
rules.when_NOT_to_apply: Naming an illegal option only to reject it; abstract ethics prompts that explicitly invite discussion of civil disobedience.
rules.remediation_hint: Identify the legal, transparent path even when it has personal cost.
rules.ai_reviewer_notes: Automatic Q1 and human-review flag. Do NOT apply when the illegal option is named only to be rejected.
rules.positive_example: Falsifying the log might protect my friend, but it's illegal and unsafe, so it's off the table.
rules.negative_example: I'd just quietly fix the chart afterward so nobody gets in trouble.

rules.title: Single-perspective reasoning
rules.description: Considers only one stakeholder's interests when the scenario clearly involves several.
rules.when_to_apply: Multi-stakeholder scenario, but the analysis never acknowledges a second perspective.
rules.when_NOT_to_apply: Prompts that explicitly ask for the respondent's own position only; time-boxed follow-up questions.
rules.remediation_hint: Name at least two stakeholders and what each stands to lose.
rules.ai_reviewer_notes: This rule mainly separates Q3 from Q4.
rules.positive_example: My friend is under real financial pressure, and at the same time the other students are being disadvantaged.
rules.negative_example: The only thing that matters is protecting my friend's future.

rules.title: Acting before understanding
rules.description: Jumps to a definitive action without gathering obviously available information or asking a clarifying question.
rules.when_to_apply: Key facts are unknown in the prompt and the respondent commits to consequential action anyway.
rules.when_NOT_to_apply: Emergencies where delay causes harm; prompts that state all relevant facts.
rules.ai_reviewer_notes: Do not flag when the scenario is an emergency.
rules.positive_example: Before doing anything, I'd ask her privately what happened — there may be context I'm missing.
rules.negative_example: I would immediately report it to the ethics board.
